summary: Milestone P0 (happy-path lifecycle) + Command Center verified end-to-end on the demo tenant. Backend: 18/18 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_p0_lifecycle.py pass in 12.7s covering: (a) POST /api/orders/extract (Claude, source_type=text) creates an order with line_items and a 'Ordine estratto' history entry; GET /api/orders/{id} returns 200 without _id leak; unknown id returns 404. (b) PUT /api/orders/{id} persists customer_name/delivery_date/notes and pushes a 'Modifiche salvate' history entry. Line-item edits (qty change on existing row + append of a new row) survive round-trip via GET. (c) POST /api/orders/{id}/validate sets status='validated' and pushes 'Ordine confermato' to history. (d) GET /api/orders/{id}/export?format=csv|excel|pdf|json — all four formats return correct MIME types (text/csv, spreadsheetml, application/pdf, application/json), correct Content-Disposition with attachment + right file extension, and valid content (xlsx begins with 'PK', pdf begins with '%PDF', json is parseable and contains order_id/line_items/total). Export flips status to 'exported' and pushes 'Esportato (FORMAT)' to history. (e) GET /api/command-center returns {today:{total,auto,review}, to_review, recent_activity, notifications, recent_customers, totals:{orders,customers}} — real aggregated data, no _id leak, demo tenant has >=1 order. Frontend (Playwright, viewport 1440x900): full happy-path recorded on the demo tenant. /app/new renders all 8 channel buttons (text/whatsapp/email/pdf/excel/csv/image/audio). Selecting 'channel-text' shows 'order-text-input' textarea; 'use-sample-order' loads a 180-char Italian sample. Clicking 'extract-order-button' shows the animated stepper (6 stages: extract-stage-0..5) and Claude extraction completes in ~10-30s, then app navigates to /app/orders/{uuid}. Order Review page renders 2-column layout: 'order-source' on the left (original Italian text visible), 5 editable line-items on the right (each with unit-price/line-total metadata), plus customer-name-input, delivery-date-input, add-line-item (verified: 5→6 rows), save-order-button (toast 'Modifiche salvate' shown), validate-order-button (toast 'Ordine confermato' shown, status flips), export-menu-button opens 'export-menu' with all 4 export testids present, and toggle-history opens 'history-panel' with 3 activity rows (Ordine estratto, Modifiche salvate x2). Zero console errors, zero pageerror events. Dashboard (/app) renders 'command-summary' with real Italian natural-language sentence ('Oggi 2 ordini · 2 in automatico · 0 da revisionare.'), 4 notification chips (notification-0..3), Da revisionare list with 2 orders (Marios (Bella Pizza), Bar Centrale), 8 activity rows (activity-order-<uuid>) including the just-created 'Ristorante Test P0', and 5 recent-customer chips (customer-<name>). Orders list (/app/orders) shows 15 rows (order-row-<uuid>), status filters (filter-all/needs_review/ready/validated/exported), search box, all working; the just-created order appears at the top with Validato status. No mocked data — the Dashboard commandsentence and Da revisionare list correctly reflect the actual /api/command-center response.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1660 lines — carry-over from iterations 4/5. Should be split into modules (auth, catalog, orders, integrations/*, dashboard/command_center). Diff review and unit-test targeting will improve materially.

GET /api/orders/{id}/export does a $set status='exported' + $push history BEFORE building the response — if PDF/Excel building throws mid-way, the DB is already mutated (status='exported' with no downloaded file). Recommend building the bytes first, then updating status only after StreamingResponse is prepared (or use a background task to update status once the download is confirmed).

PDF generator does not HTML-escape order['notes'] or order['customer_name'] before passing to reportlab.Paragraph — a customer name containing '<' or '&' will either break rendering or be interpreted as markup. Wrap with xml.sax.saxutils.escape or reportlab's Paragraph.escape. Low probability but easy fix.

history_entry lacks a timestamp field visible to the client — the frontend History panel shows dates that must be inferred from array position; consider always attaching an ISO timestamp to each history entry for reliable ordering when concurrent PUTs happen.
WhatsApp webhook still does not verify X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC (carried over from iterations 4/5). HIGH before enabling real Meta creds in production.
No unique compound Mongo index on (company_id, external_id) — concurrent webhook retries can double-insert (carried over from iteration 4/5).
In pilot mode the /login page is still reachable and unlabelled — minor UX polish (carried over from iteration 5).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_p0_lifecycle.py (new — 18 tests across 6 classes: TestExtractAndGet, TestUpdate, TestValidate, TestExport, TestCommandCenter, TestOrdersList, TestLineItemEdit)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 13 happy-path checkpoints incl. stepper, 4 export testids, history panel, dashboard summary, orders list filters)

test_credentials: Demo tenant: demo@ordia.app / demo123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — auto-loaded via pilot mode). Fresh isolation tenants: test_p0_<hex>@example.com / testpass123.
seed_data_creation: Per run: 1 fresh @example.com company via /api/auth/register + 1 order created via /api/orders/extract on it (mutated with a TEST_added row + qty edit + customer='Ristorante Test P0'). On the demo tenant, one additional order is created + validated + exported through the Playwright flow (Ristorante Test P0, 6 lines) — harmless and now visible in Command Center. No fixture cleanup needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_p0_lifecycle.py is the P0 happy-path regression suite. It reads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env directly. Extraction uses Claude via emergentintegrations — allow up to 90s per extract call. All 4 export formats (csv, excel/xlsx, pdf, json) are verified by MIME + Content-Disposition + magic bytes. Command Center response shape is asserted for {today, to_review, recent_activity, notifications, recent_customers, totals}. The demo tenant currently holds ~15 orders including 2 whatsapp-source ones from iter4 fixtures. Frontend testids to reuse: channel-{text|whatsapp|email|pdf|excel|csv|image|audio}, extract-stage-0..5, order-source, line-item-<id>, customer-name-input, delivery-date-input, add-line-item, duplicate-item-<id>, remove-item-<id>, match-select-<id>, product-option-<id>, qty-input-<id>, unit-input-<id>, save-order-button, validate-order-button, export-menu-button, export-menu, export-{pdf|excel|csv|json}-button, toggle-history, history-panel, command-summary, notification-0..N, review-order-<uuid>, activity-order-<uuid>, customer-<name>, order-row-<uuid>, filter-{all|needs_review|ready|validated|exported}. Not tested by design (per review request): AI suggestion chips (suggestion-<itemId>-<productId>) — will only render when Claude leaves an item unmatched; on this run all 5 lines matched with 82-99% confidence. Also not tested (P1 per review request): clicking a recent-customer chip (route not built).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All P0 acceptance criteria from the review request are met — 8 channels render, text extract → 6-stage stepper → navigate to review, 2-column editor with all edit operations, save/validate toasts, 4-format export with correct MIME/magic bytes/history entries, history panel with Italian action names, and Command Center with real aggregated data (no hardcoded values). The 7 items in critical_code_review_comments are pre-existing tech debt or future-hardening items, none block the P0 milestone.
